creator: nemysis@gmx.ch
text:
Additional Player Levels for Rocks'n'Diamonds and R'n'D jue.

WWW: http://www.bd-fans.com/RnD.html

Generated and tested manually, tested with port test, sent with FreeBSD Port Tools 0.99_6 (mode: new)

How-To-Repeat: 
portlint -A
WARN: Makefile: no port directory /usr/ports/games/rnd_jue found, even though it is listed in RUN_DEPENDS.
WARN: /var/tmp/ports/usr/local/ports/local/work/games/rocksndiamonds-data/work/pkg-message: possible use of absolute pathname "/var/tmp/ports/rocks...".
WARN: /var/tmp/ports/usr/local/ports/local/work/games/rocksndiamonds-data/work/pkg-message: possible use of absolute pathname "/var/tmp/ports/rocks...".
0 fatal errors and 3 warnings found.

WARN: Makefile: because isn't in FreeBSD Ports yet
games/rnd_jue
http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=174747


WARN: /pkg-message: possible use of absolute pathname, because is used

find -L %%PREFIX%%/share/rocksndiamonds -type l -exec rm -- {} +
find -L %%PREFIX%%/share/rnd_jue -type l -exec rm -- {} +

to delete bad symlinks when this Ports is deinstaled.


port test
...
ln: /var/tmp/ports/rocksndiamonds-data-20121227/share/rocksndiamonds/levels: No such file or directory, because is used

.for l in ${LEVELS_ARTSOFT} ${LEVELS_BD_FANS} Puzzles
    @${LN} -sf ${DATADIR}/levels/${l} ${DATADIR1}/levels
.endfor
    @${LN} -sf ${DATADIR}/levels/Tutorials/rnd_tutorial_alpha ${DATADIR1}/levels/Tutorials

And is used to define

DATADIR=	${PREFIX}/share/...
DATADIR1=	${PREFIX}/share/...

In native FreeBSD works port test clean.
